title: taraxacum fabrei
intro: Taraxacum fabrei, also known as the Fabre's dandelion, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum fabrei are attributed to its rich content of inulin, a type of fructan with potential prebiotic properties. Research has shown that the plant may offer health benefits, including the improvement of digestive health and the regulation of blood sugar levels. Taraxacum fabrei can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, or capsules, allowing for various forms of consumption.
